assertions.title: Task creation

steps.action: Run: cargo run --bin swell -- task "hello world"
steps.expected: CLI creates task, returns task ID
steps.observed: CLI panicked: Invalid response format: Error("expected value", line: 1, column: 1)

evidence.network: Daemon receives command (verified via nc), but CLI cannot parse response

assertions.issues: CLI fails to parse daemon response - gets empty/corrupted data when reading from Unix socket. The CLI uses read_buf into a pre-allocated vector, but the response is empty or malformed. Daemon works correctly via raw nc (verified TaskCreate and TaskList work).

assertions.title: Task listing

steps.observed: CLI panicked: Invalid response format: Error("expected value", line: 1, column: 1)

evidence.network: nc proves daemon returns valid JSON: {"type":"TaskCompleted","payload":{...}}

assertions.issues: Same issue as VAL-CLI-001 - CLI cannot parse daemon response. Verified via nc that daemon returns correct JSON but CLI read_buf returns empty/corrupted data.

assertions.title: Task watch
assertions.issues: Skipped - depends on CLI ability to communicate with daemon which is broken (same underlying bug as VAL-CLI-001/002)

assertions.title: Task approve/cancel
assertions.issues: Skipped - depends on CLI ability to communicate with daemon which is broken (same underlying bug as VAL-CLI-001/002)

steps.action: Start daemon with cargo run --bin swell-daemon
Send TaskCreate via nc: printf '{"type":"TaskCreate","payload":{"description":"test"}}\n' | nc -U /tmp/swell-daemon.sock
steps.expected: Valid JSON response with task ID

evidence.network: Daemon returns structured error for invalid commands

frictions.description: nc sends JSON without newline terminator at end of transmission, daemon processes it correctly but nc shows concatenated output when multiple commands sent
frictions.resolution: Used single command per nc invocation

frictions.description: Multiple daemon instances were running simultaneously from earlier attempts, causing confusion about which instance was handling requests
frictions.resolution: Killed all daemon processes and restarted fresh

blockers.description: CLI cannot read responses from daemon Unix socket - read_buf returns empty data causing JSON parse failure. This blocks all CLI assertions (VAL-CLI-001 to VAL-CLI-004)
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified daemon works correctly via nc and Python socket test. The issue is in CLI's read_buf implementation. Tried rebuilding binaries - issue persists. Issue appears to be how CLI reads from Unix socket - daemon response is valid but CLI gets empty buffer.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-DAEMON-001, VAL-DAEMON-003), 2 failed (VAL-CLI-001, VAL-CLI-002 - CLI cannot parse daemon responses), 2 skipped (VAL-CLI-003, VAL-CLI-004 - depend on working CLI). The daemon correctly implements Unix socket communication and error handling as verified via nc/Python. However, the CLI client has a bug where it cannot read responses from the Unix socket - read_buf returns empty data causing panic in JSON parsing. This is a bug in swell-cli/src/main.rs at line 85 in the send_command function.
